notes: Gene sets with fewer detected genes are recorded as unavailable rather than tested.
Bins below this threshold are excluded from event-FDR and reported as low identifiability.
Adaptive windows may use 100-500 cells; selected values are recorded in output manifests.
Span is expressed on normalized pseudotime from 0 to 1.
Adjacent windows overlap unless an analysis is explicitly time-bin based.
Covariate standardized mean differences above this threshold trigger downgrade or low-overlap status.
Samples, embryos, donors, libraries or guide blocks are counted as blocks depending on design.
High-priority family analyses may use more permutations; GSE271399 family block FDR used 5000.
Recorded in gse271399_family_block_permutation_fdr.tsv.

scope: Block confidence intervals and stability summaries
notes: Used when raw block-level effects are available; held-out summaries are used for adapter datasets.
Events above this threshold may remain descriptive or scaffold-level candidates.
Must be combined with direction stability or confidence interval support.
Fraction of bootstrap, held-out or variant estimates with the primary direction.
Primary event effect must exceed matched random, housekeeping, stress/proliferation or not-comparable controls.
If matched-state adjustment removes at least half of the raw effect, the event is downgraded or labeled artifact-sensitive.
Perturbed peak must shift later by at least one ordered time/pseudotime bin.
Late partial recovery above this fraction supports delay rather than permanent loss.
Overlapping gene sets above this Jaccard threshold can be merged if event direction is concordant.
Dynamic profiles above this correlation can be grouped into the same event family.
Below this product score, absence of signal is interpreted as not identifiable rather than negative.
Above this product score, event calls can pass regular boundary gates if other evidence is present.
